Article.ArticleTitle: Distal Radial Approach through the Anatomical Snuff Box for Coronary Angiography and Percutaneous Coronary Intervention.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Division of Cardiology, Department of Internal Medicine, Chungnam National University Hospital, Chungnam National University School of Medicine, Daejeon, Korea.
Division of Cardiology, Department of Internal Medicine, Chungnam National University Hospital, Chungnam National University School of Medicine, Daejeon, Korea. myheart@cnu.ac.kr.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors have no financial conflicts of interest.

Reference.Citation: Pyles ST, Scher KS. Cannulation of the radial artery in the anatomic snuffbox. Surg Gynecol Obstet. 1983;156:227–228.
Deepika K, Palaniappan D, Fuhrman T, Saltzmanm B. Anatomic snuffbox radial artery cannulation. Anesth Analg. 2010;111:1078–1079.
Kiemeneij F. Left distal transradial access in the anatomical snuffbox for coronary angiography (ldTRA) and interventions (ldTRI) EuroIntervention. 2017;13:851–857.
Pua U, Quek LHH. “Snuffbox” distal radial access. J Vasc Interv Radiol. 2018;29:44.
Kim Y, Ahn Y, Kim I, et al. Feasibility of coronary angiography and percutaneous coronary intervention via left snuffbox approach. Korean Circ J. 2018;48:1120–1130.

Kim Y, Jeong MH, Kim I, et al. Intravascular ultrasound-guided percutaneous coronary intervention with drug-eluting stent for unprotected left main disease via left snuffbox approach. Korean Circ J. 2018;48:532–533.
Jang HJ, Kim JY, Han JD, et al. Numbness after transradial cardiac catheterization: the results from a nerve conduction study of the superficial radial nerve. Korean Circ J. 2016;46:161–168.
